tools.name: create_test_case
tools.title: Create Test Case
tools.description: Create a new test case in Allure TestOps.

name.description: The name of the test case.

description.description: A markdown description of the test case.

steps.description: List of steps. Each step must be a dict with 'action' and 'expected' keys. Example: [{'action': 'Login', 'expected': 'Dashboard visible'}]

custom_fields.description: Dictionary of custom field names and their values (string or list of strings).Example: {'Layer': 'UI', 'Components': ['Auth', 'DB']}

test_layer_id.description: Optional test layer ID to assign (use list_test_layers to find IDs). If provided, the layer must exist in the project.

test_layer_name.description: Optional test layer name to assign (exact case-sensitive match). Mutually exclusive with test_layer_id.

issues.description: Optional list of issue keys to link (e.g., ['PROJ-123']).

integration_id.description: Optional integration ID for issue linking (use list_integrations to find IDs). Required when multiple integrations exist. Mutually exclusive with integration_name.

integration_name.description: Optional integration name for issue linking (exact case-sensitive match). Required when multiple integrations exist. Mutually exclusive with integration_id.

project_id.description: Optional override for the default Project ID.

tools.name: get_test_case_details
tools.title: Get Test Case Details
tools.description:
Get complete details of a specific test case.

Retrieves all information about a test case including its steps, tags,
custom fields, and attachments. Use this before updating a test case to
understand its current state.

test_case_id.description: ID of the test case to retrieve.

project_id.description: Allure TestOps project ID to list test cases from.

CustomFieldEntry.description: A named custom-field value exposed by test-case details.
CustomFieldEntry.title: CustomFieldEntry

Step.description: A serialized scenario step, including recursive shared-step children.

expected.description: Expected result for an inline step.
expected.title: Expected

Step.title: Step

outputSchema.description: Structured details for one test case.
outputSchema.title: TestCaseDetailsOutput

tools.name: update_test_case
tools.title: Update Test Case
tools.description:
Update an existing test case in Allure TestOps.
⚠️ CAUTION: Destructive.

Performs a partial update: only supplied fields are sent to the API. When
provided, ``steps`` replace all existing steps, and ``attachments`` replace
all existing global attachments. Omit a field to preserve its current value.

test_case_id.description: The ID of the test case to update

name.description: New name for the test case

steps.description: New list of steps. Each step is a dict with 'action', 'expected', and optional 'attachments' list.

attachments.description: New list of global attachments. Each dict has 'name' and 'content' (base64) or 'url'.

custom_fields.description: Dictionary of custom fields to update (Name -> Value or list of values)

automated.description: Set whether the test case is automated

expected_result.description: Global expected result for the test case

status_id.description: ID of the test case status

links.description: New list of external links. Each dict has 'name', 'url', and optional 'type'.

issues.description: List of issue keys to ADD (e.g. ['PROJ-123']).

remove_issues.description: List of issue keys to REMOVE.

clear_issues.description: If True, remove ALL issues from the test case.

integration_id.description: Optional integration ID for issue linking (use list_integrations to find IDs). Required when multiple integrations exist and adding issues. Mutually exclusive with integration_name.

integration_name.description: Optional integration name for issue linking (exact case-sensitive match). Required when multiple integrations exist and adding issues. Mutually exclusive with integration_id.

project_id.description: Optional override for the default Project ID.

confirm.description: Must be set to True to proceed with update. Safety measure.

tools.name: delete_test_case
tools.title: Delete Test Case
tools.description:
Archive an obsolete test case.
⚠️ CAUTION: Destructive.

This performs a SOFT DELETE (archive). The test case can typically
be recovered from the Allure UI if needed.

⚠️ CAUTION: This action removes the test case from active views.
Historical data and launch associations may be affected.

test_case_id.description: The Allure test case ID to archive.

confirm.description: Must be set to True to proceed with deletion. Safety measure.

project_id.description: Optional Allure TestOps project ID override.

tools.name: delete_archived_test_cases
tools.title: Delete Archived Test Cases
tools.description: Permanently delete all archived/deleted test cases in the current project.

confirm.description: Must be set to True to proceed.

project_id.description: Optional Allure TestOps project ID override.

tools.name: search_test_cases
tools.title: Search Test Cases
tools.description:
Search for test cases by name, tag, or AQL query.

Find test cases matching your search criteria. Supports simple name/tag search
or advanced AQL (Allure Query Language) for complex filtering.

Simple Query Syntax (use 'query' parameter):
- Plain text: Searches in test case names (case-insensitive)
- tag:value: Filters by exact tag match
- Combined: "login tag:smoke" finds test cases with "login" in name AND "smoke" tag

Important: see https://docs.qameta.io/allure-testops/advanced/aql/ for the full AQL syntax reference.
AQL Syntax (use 'aql' parameter):
- Operators: and, or, not
- Precedence: and binds tighter than or; use parentheses to group
- Strings are double-quoted; numbers are unquoted; booleans are true/false
- Comparison operators: =, !=, ~= (contains), in, not in
- Field examples: status, tag, name, createdBy, automated, layer

query.description: Simple search query. Examples: 'login flow', 'tag:smoke', 'tag:smoke tag:regression', 'authentication tag:security'. Ignored when 'aql' is provided.

aql.description:
Raw AQL (Allure Query Language) for complex searches. Important: see https://docs.qameta.io/allure-testops/advanced/aql/ for the full AQL syntax reference.Supports AND, OR, NOT operators, parentheses for grouping, and field filters. Strings must be double-quoted. Examples:
  - 'status="failed" and tag="regression"'
  - '(createdBy = "John" or createdBy = "Jane") and name ~= "test"'
  - 'tag in ["smoke", "e2e"] and automated = true'
  - 'not status = "Draft" and layer = "API"'

project_id.description: Allure TestOps project ID to list test cases from.

items.description: Matching test cases.
A compact test-case entry returned by search and list tools.
items.title: TestCaseSummary
Items

manual_only.description: When true, return only manual results. Filtering is handled for you.

page.description: Zero-based page index after optional filtering.

tools.name: rerun_test_results_manually
tools.title: Rerun Test Results Manually
tools.description: Schedule manual reruns for selected launch results.

launch_id.description: Launch ID containing the failed results (required).

result_ids.description: One or more launch result IDs to schedule for manual rerun.

assignees.description: Optional usernames to assign during manual rerun scheduling.

force_manual.description: Force manual rerun mode when the upstream API supports it.

project_id.description: Optional override for the default Project ID.

tools.name: start_manual_test_session
tools.title: Start Manual Test Session
tools.description: Start a manual execution session for a launch.

project_id.description: Optional override for the default Project ID.

items.description: A string key/value pair, for example a manual-session environment entry.
items.title: KeyValue

tools.name: submit_manual_test_results
tools.title: Submit Manual Test Results
tools.description: Submit manual execution results for a manual session.

results.description: Manual result payloads. When an item includes result_id from list_launch_test_results, the service resolves that existing launch result in place through TestOps' test-result run controller. After rerun_test_results_manually, re-list launch results and submit against the newly visible active result for that test case. The returned result_ids are the resolved result IDs to use for follow-up attachments and reads. As a lower-level fallback, you may still provide launch_id + test_case_id + name/full_name explicitly to create a standalone manual result. Optional fields include status/start/stop/duration/message/trace/description/precondition/expected_result/steps.

project_id.description: Optional override for the default Project ID.

tools.name: upload_test_results
tools.title: Upload Test Results
tools.description: Upload external test results to an existing launch.

launch_id.description: Launch ID to receive the results (required).

results.description: Result objects to append to the launch. Every item requires test_case_id (int) and status (passed, failed, broken, skipped, or unknown). Optional fields: start, stop, duration, message, name, and full_name. A maximum of 1000 results is accepted per call.

project_id.description: Optional override for the default Project ID.

items.description: A rejected bulk-operation entry.
items.title: Failure

step_index.description: Optional zero-based manual step index to resolve within the test result execution.

fixture_result_id.description: Optional explicit fixture result ID for legacy fixture-step fallback.

fixture_name.description: Optional fixture name used only for the legacy fixture-step fallback.

fixture_type.description: Optional fixture type hint for the legacy fixture-step fallback: 'before' or 'after'.

tools.name: update_shared_step
tools.title: Update Shared Step
tools.description:
Update an existing shared step.
⚠️ CAUTION: Destructive.

⚠️ IMPORTANT: Changes propagate to ALL test cases using this shared step.

Only provided fields will be updated. Omitted fields remain unchanged.
Repeated calls with the same data are idempotent.

step_id.description: The shared step ID to update (required).

name.description: New name for the shared step (optional).

confirm.description: Must be set to True to proceed with update. Safety measure.

project_id.description: Optional override for the default Project ID.

tools.name: delete_shared_step
tools.title: Delete Shared Step
tools.description:
Delete a shared step from the library.
⚠️ CAUTION: Destructive.

⚠️ CAUTION: If this shared step is used by test cases, deleting it
will break those references.

step_id.description: The shared step ID to delete (required).

confirm.description: Must be True to proceed (safety measure).

project_id.description: Optional override for the default Project ID.

tools.name: link_shared_step

tools.description:
Link a shared step to a test case.
⚠️ CAUTION: Destructive.

Adds a reference to the shared step in the test case's step list.
The shared step's actions will expand at execution time.

test_case_id.description: The ID of test case to modify.

shared_step_id.description: The ID of shared step to link.

position.description: Where to insert the shared step (0-indexed, optional). 0 = Insert at beginning None = Append to end (default) N = Insert after step N (so it becomes the (N+1)th step)

project_id.description: Optional override for the default Project ID.

confirm.description: Must be set to True to proceed with linking. Safety measure.

expected.description: Expected result for an inline step.

tools.name: create_test_plan
tools.title: Create Test Plan
tools.description:
Create a new Test Plan in Allure TestOps.

This tool allows creating a Test Plan, which is a collection of Test Cases
to be executed. You can define the content of the plan either by:
1. Explicitly listing Test Case IDs (`test_case_ids`).
2. Providing an AQL (Allure Query Language) filter (`aql_filter`).
3. Both (explicit selection + dynamic filter).

test_case_ids.description: List of Test Case IDs to include

aql_filter.description: AQL query to select test cases

tools.name: update_test_plan
tools.title: Update Test Plan
tools.description:
Update the metadata of an existing Test Plan.

Currently, supports updating the name of the plan.

tools.name: manage_test_plan_content
tools.title: Manage Test Plan Content
tools.description:
Modify the content (Test Cases) of an existing Test Plan.

Allows adding or removing specific Test Cases by ID, or updating the
underlying AQL filter query.

add_test_case_ids.description: List of Test Case IDs to add

remove_test_case_ids.description: List of Test Case IDs to remove

tools.name: list_test_plans
tools.title: List Test Plans
tools.description:
List Test Plans for the current project.

Retrieves a paginated list of Test Plans, showing their IDs, names,
and the number of test cases they contain.

Delete a Test Plan.
⚠️ CAUTION: Destructive.

Permanently removes the Test Plan from Allure TestOps.
The operation is idempotent: if the plan does not exist, it returns success.

plan_id.description: ID of the test plan to delete

confirm.description: Must be set to True to proceed with deletion. Safety measure.

tools.name: create_defect
tools.title: Create Defect
tools.description:
Create a new defect in the current project.

Use this tool to register a known defect that can later be linked
to failing test results through defect matchers (automation rules).

tools.name: get_defect
tools.title: Get Defect
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific defect.

defect_id.description: ID of the defect to retrieve

tools.name: link_defect_to_test_case
tools.title: Link Defect To Test Case
tools.description:
Link a defect to a test case through a shared issue mapping.

This operation ensures defect governance and test coverage are connected:
the issue is linked to the defect and to the specified test case.

defect_id.description: ID of the defect to link

test_case_id.description: ID of the test case to link

issue_key.description: Issue key to use for linking (for example, PROJ-123). If omitted, the defect's existing issue mapping is reused.

integration_id.description: Optional integration ID for issue mapping. Mutually exclusive with integration_name.

integration_name.description: Optional integration name for issue mapping. Mutually exclusive with integration_id.

tools.name: list_defect_test_cases
tools.title: List Defect Test Cases
tools.description: List test cases currently linked to a defect.

defect_id.description: ID of the defect whose linked test cases should be listed

items.description: Closed common vocabulary for collection entries across Lucius tools.
items.title: EntitySummary

items.description: Entity-specific collection entries.
items.title: Items

tools.name: unlink_issue_from_test_case
tools.title: Unlink Issue From Test Case
tools.description:
Unlink an issue from a test case.

This operation is idempotent: if the issue is already unlinked, it still
returns a successful confirmation. Supply the issue key (for example,
``PROJ-123``) or the internal issue-link ID returned by Allure TestOps.

test_case_id.description: ID of the test case to unlink from

issue_id.description: Issue key or internal issue-link ID to unlink

outputSchema.description: Confirmation for unlinking an issue by numeric ID or issue key.
outputSchema.title: UnlinkIssueFromTestCaseOutput

tools.name: update_defect
tools.title: Update Defect
tools.description:
Update an existing defect's name, description, or status.

At least one field must be provided. Fields set to null/None are
left unchanged.

defect_id.description: ID of the defect to update

closed.description: Set to true to close, false to reopen

tools.name: delete_defect
tools.title: Delete Defect
tools.description:
Permanently delete a defect and all its associated matchers.

This is a destructive operation. The ``confirm`` parameter must be
set to ``true`` to actually perform the deletion.

defect_id.description: ID of the defect to delete

confirm.description: Safety flag — must be set to true to confirm deletion

tools.name: list_defects
tools.title: List Defects
tools.description: List all defects in the current project.

items.description: Closed common vocabulary for collection entries across Lucius tools.
items.title: EntitySummary

items.description: Entity-specific collection entries.
items.title: Items

tools.name: create_defect_matcher
tools.title: Create Defect Matcher
tools.description:
Create a defect matcher (automation rule) for a defect.

Matchers automatically link future failing test results to a defect
when the test failure's error message or stack trace matches the
provided regex patterns.

At least one of ``message_regex`` or ``trace_regex`` must be
supplied.

message_regex.description: Regex to match against error messages

trace_regex.description: Regex to match against stack traces

tools.name: update_defect_matcher
tools.title: Update Defect Matcher
tools.description:
Update a defect matcher's name or regex patterns.

At least one field must be provided.

matcher_id.description: ID of the matcher to update

tools.name: delete_defect_matcher
tools.title: Delete Defect Matcher
tools.description:
Permanently delete a defect matcher (automation rule).

This is a destructive operation. The ``confirm`` parameter must be
set to ``true`` to actually perform the deletion.

matcher_id.description: ID of the matcher to delete

confirm.description: Safety flag — must be set to true to confirm deletion

tools.name: list_defect_matchers
tools.title: List Defect Matchers
tools.description: List all matchers (automation rules) for a given defect.

items.description: A matcher entry returned when listing a defect's automation rules.
items.title: DefectMatcherSummary
